scenario: The right skill, the moment you need it

explainer.title: Here's your whole idea on one page.
explainer.body: We mapped one moment: the concierge handing you the right skill while you're brainstorming ads. Across the top is the journey, start to finish. Down the side is everything that has to exist for it to work, what you tap, what runs behind the scenes, the rules you set, where it could break, and why. The grey steps are the behind-the-scenes parts you never actually see.
explainer.read: Read left → right for the journey. Read top → bottom to see everything standing behind each step.

steps.name: Connect your skills
steps.def: Imports the skills you already installed

steps.name: Learn your work
steps.def: Quietly notices what you tend to do

steps.name: You hit a moment
steps.def: You start a task, like brainstorming ads

steps.name: Find the right skill
steps.def: Matches the moment to a skill that helps

steps.name: The tap on the shoulder
steps.def: Surfaces one skill, right in the chat

steps.name: You use it
steps.def: One tap and the skill is on

steps.name: It gets smarter
steps.def: Learns from your yes or no

touchpoint.1: Your skills, all in one list
touchpoint.3: Whatever you're working in (a Claude chat)
touchpoint.5: A quiet suggestion card in the chat

process.2: Watch patterns in what you do
process.4: Rank by fit + popularity + your past yes/no

policy.2: Your activity stays private, on your machine
policy.5: One skill at a time · never twice in a row
policy.6: Always one tap to dismiss or mute

pitfall.2: Feels creepy if it watches too closely
pitfall.4: Suggests the wrong skill → you lose trust
pitfall.5: Nudges too often → the annoying paperclip
pitfall.7: Learns the wrong lesson from one bad day

rationale.1: Start from what you already have, not a blank slate
rationale.4: Simple ranking first; your feedback makes it smart
rationale.5: One quiet card respects your focus
rationale.7: Your yes/no is the best signal there is

question.2: How much watching is helpful vs. creepy?
question.4: How do we rank tens or hundreds of skills fairly?
question.5: When is the right moment to speak up?
question.7: How fast should it adapt to your feedback?

note.1: Most people forget half of what they installed
note.3: Ad brainstorming is a perfect trigger moment
note.4: Lean on install counts, ratings, freshness
